name: Dysprosium Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Dysprosium. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Dysprosium oxide removal without substrate damage

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength optimized for Dysprosium's absorption characteristics

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise cleaning control

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for continuous cleaning coverage

fluenceThreshold.description: Minimum fluence required for effective Dysprosium oxide removal

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for efficient oxide layer ablation

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform cleaning

passCount.description: Recommended number of passes for thorough cleaning

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap for complete surface coverage

power.description: Typical range for industrial laser cleaning of reflective metals like Dysprosium at 1064 nm to ablate contaminants without substrate melting (melting point ~1412°C). Starting at 200 W balances efficiency and safety; higher risks thermal damage due to moderate thermal conductivity (~10.7 W/m·K). Source: General Nd:YAG laser specs from IPG Photonics and studies on rare earth metal processing (e.g., J. Appl. Phys. on lanthanide ablation).

scan_speed.description: Effective speeds for contaminant removal on Dysprosium; slower for heavy contamination (e.g., oxides), faster for light. Recommended 1500 mm/s optimizes fluence delivery without excessive heat buildup. Conservative for uniform coverage on soft, reflective surfaces. Source: Industry standards from laser cleaning manuals (e.g., Adapt Laser Systems) and process optimization in Opt. Lasers Eng.

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for cost-effective cleaning of Dysprosium; picosecond (ps) or femtosecond reduces HAZ but increases cost. 20 ns minimizes thermal diffusion in low-conductivity metal while ablating surface layers. Source: Laser physics research on metal ablation thresholds (e.g., Appl. Surf. Sci. reviews on ns vs. ps for rare earths).

repetition_rate.description: Standard for pulsed cleaning to achieve high throughput; 50 kHz recommended for Dysprosium to balance speed and avoid heat accumulation on reflective surfaces (high reflectivity ~70% at 1064 nm). Higher rates increase efficiency but risk subsurface heating. Source: Academic literature on pulse train effects (e.g., J. Phys. D: Appl. Phys. on metal cleaning).

energy_density.description: Fluence range for safe contaminant removal; Dysprosium damage threshold estimated ~2-4 J/cm² based on similar lanthanides. Recommended 1.5 J/cm² for cleaning without ablation of base metal, considering reflectivity and thermal properties. Conservative due to limited specific data. Source: Ablation threshold studies (e.g., Phys. Rev. B on rare earth metals; general metal cleaning in Laser Ablation and Its Applications).

spot_size.description: Beam diameter for precise industrial cleaning; smaller for fine details on Dysprosium components, larger for broad areas. 200 μm recommended for balanced energy distribution and minimal distortion on soft metal. Source: Equipment specifications from Coherent and Trumpf lasers for metal surface prep.

pass_count.description: Single pass for light contamination, multi-pass for heavy; 2 passes recommended for complete Dysprosium cleaning to ensure uniformity without over-processing reflective surfaces. Source: Industry case studies on iterative laser cleaning (e.g., Proc. SPIE on multi-pass optimization for metals).

overlap_ratio.description: Ensures uniform coverage; 60% recommended for Dysprosium to avoid gaps or streaks on large areas while preventing excessive overlap heating. Typical range from process studies. Source: Optimization studies in J. Laser Appl. and laser scanning guidelines from scanning head manufacturers like Scanlab.
